## Sentiment

Rajdeep, KaurKaur, MunishChahalSaini *Department**of**Computer**Science,**Guru**Nanak* *Department**of**Computer**Engineering**and**Technology, Guru* *Amritsar,**India* [rajdeep.rsh@gndu.ac.in](mailto:rajdeep.rsh@gndu.ac.in), [kuljitchahal.cse@gndu.ac.in](mailto:kuljitchahal.cse@gndu.ac.in),

**Abstract** **Background:**In theopensourcesoftwareparadigm, uponefforts of volunteer members that are geographically dispersed and collaborate with each otherovertheInternet.Communication issue tracking systemsareusedbydevelopers themselves throughthesecommunicationchannels efficiency ofdevelopmentactivities,andsurvival is essentialtounderstandaffectivestateof engineering moreeffective. **Aim:**This study examined commit logsofseven GitHub projects sentiments. Thisstudyalsoinvestigatedthe commit logswithteamsizeofproject,typeof **Method:**Sentiments ofdevelopersarecalculated specialized insoftware engineeringdomain. **Results:**Our findingsrevealedthatthemajorityof in commit logs were neutral. Furthermore, we found that team size, change activity, and commit contributionvolumeinfluencedsentiments conveyed in **Conclusion:**Our findingswillhelpprojectmanagers sentiments whileperformingdifferentsoftware beneficial inimprovingdeveloperproductivity

**Keywords:**human factorsinsoftwaredevelopment developers’ sentiment,sentimentanalysis, and teamsize

**1. Introduction**

Sentimentsof software developers greatly influence the quality and productivity of developed software[1]. Prior studies confirm that emotions impact task quality, productivity, grouprapport, and job satisfaction [2]. Due to advancements (NLP)and significance of human computer interaction, research associated with sentiments and emotionalaspectsofsoftwaredevelopers’ the softwareengineeringdomain.

**2. Related**literature

Manystudies have beenconducted byresearchers andpractitioners inthepasttoanalyze the developers’sentiments inOSScoderepositoriesandrelatedartifacts.Theyexamined developers’sentiments in different software artifacts such as commit logs,comments, mailinglist messages, and GitHub security debates. Asummary ofthe related literature is presented inTable 1. Some researchersevaluatedtheperformanceofSE-specificsentimentanalysistools, comparedthem in terms of accuracy, and proposed techniques to improve existing sentiment analysis tools.Noviellietal.[16]in2021presentedareplicationstudytoevaluatethe performance ofSE-specifictools.Sunetal.[17]proposedsentencestructuretoimprove sentiment analysisinsoftwareengineeringtext.Biswasetal.[18]in2020,investigated the effectivenessofacustomizedlanguagerepresentationmodelknownasBERTand Noviellietal.[19] assessed theperformance offour SEdomain specific tools viz. Senti4SD, SentiCR, SentiStrength-SE,andDEVA incross-platform.M.R.Wrobel[20]investigated the influenceofadoptionoflexiconsonemotionmininginSEartifacts. In theyear2021,MartinObaidiandJilKlünder[21]presentedasystematicliterature reviewof sentiment analysis tools designed for and applied in a software engineering context. Thisstudy explored sentiments analysis tools used in the software engineering field, utilized data sets,applicationareasofsentimentanalysistools,andproblemsfacedatthetimeof developing such kindsoftools. Some researchersexploredthesentimentvariationbasedondifferentfactorsandalso examinedthe association of sentiments with various factors. Huq et al. [22] in 2020 therelation of sentiments with software bugs. In the same year, Kaur and Chahal presented investigation ofdevelopers’sentimentsincommitcomments[23].Intheyear2019,Paul et al.[11]analyzedthecodereviewdataoffiveopensourceprojectstoinvestigatethe difference inexpressionofsentimentsbasedonthegenderofdevelopersduringvarious softwaredevelopment tasks.Intheyear2018,BhartiandSingh[24]surveyed 20 professionalstoexamine thedevelopers’ sentiments associated withcodecloning practices. Islamand Zibran [7] studied the variance in emotion in commit messages that are related to bugintroduction andbugfixing activities. Singh etal.[5]have analyzed the3,171 commit messagesthatarerelatedtorefactoring activities toinvestigate theimpactof15different coderefactoring tasks ondevelopers’ sentiments. This study identified that the sentimentsaremorenegativeduringrefactoringactivities.SouzaandSilva [25]examined the relationshipbetweensentimentsofdevelopersandbuildbreakageinaTravisCI (continuousintegration). Sinha et al. [5] investigated the developer sentiment in the commit logsofGitHub projects and studied the association among developer sentiment and day of theweek. They also examined the correlation between developer sentiment and the number of fileschangesperformedbythedeveloperinthecommits.Thisstudydemonstrates that mostofthesentimentsprojectedbydevelopersinthecommitlogwereneutral.The negative sentimentsare10%higherthanthepositiveandthemajorityofthe sentimentwasdetectedonTuesday. Islamand Zibran [13] investigated sentiments variation based on different types of tasks executedby developers, developmentperiod, in different size projects, and impact of emotions on softwareartifacts(i.e.,lengthofcommitmessage).Garciaetal.[12]analyzedthedata of thebugtrackingsystemandmailinglisttoexaminetheassociationbetweenemotions and contributor activity. Guzman etal.[4]examinedcommitcommentsofGitHubprojectstoinvestigatethe relation ofdevelopersentimentwiththeprogramminglanguageusedbytheproject,time

**3.1. Data**

GitHub collaborative extracted size, 10 years), projects have creation of the

etal.e-InformaticaSoftware EngineeringJournal,16(2022),220102

etal.[8]presented astudy toinvestigate thepresence ofpositive andnegative inuseranddeveloper mailinglists.Thisstudyfoundthatbothtypesof positiveaswellasnegativesentimentsandhaveadifferentfocus. et al. [26] examined sentimentsassociated with security discussions in commits and requests. This study identified that negative emotions are higher in security debates in to non-securitydiscussions. Khan et al. [27] have analyzed the impact of emotions debuggingperformanceofsoftwareprogrammers.MüllerandFritz[28]presented

betweendeveloperemotionandtheirabilitytosolveanalyticalproblems. found that happy software developers are better at solving analytical problems. In the 2013, Wrobel [30] presented a study on developers’ emotions in the software development asurvey. bestofourknowledge,theworkpresentedinthepastdoesnotexplorethe teamsizeoftheproject,typeofchangeactivity,andcommitcontribution ofdevelopers. The work presented inthis paper ismotivated by Sinha etal. [5]. investigatedtherelationofthedayofweekandnumberofchangedfileswith sentiments. Butthisstudy doesnotexplore theassociation ofcombinations of typeandcommitcontributionwithdevelopers’sentiments.Oneanother byGuzmanetal.[4]examinedthesentimentsexpressedbydevelopers commentsandinvestigate theirassociationwithdifferentfactorsliketime projectapproval, codinglanguage,andteamgeographicaldistribution.But notconsidertheteamsizeoftheprojectanditsassociationwithdevelopers’ Thuslackofresearchinthefieldmotivatedustoconductthis workexaminedthewholecommitlogsofsevenGitHubprojectstoanalyze softwaredevelopersprojectedincommitlogsandinvestigatetheeffect size, typeofchange activity, andcommit contribution onthedevelopers’ sentiments. alsolookintotheevolutionofsentimentstoidentifyhowthesechanges theyears along with thenumber ofcommits. We utilized theSentiStrength-SE tool sentiment analysis. We selected thistoolbecauseitisthefirstdomain-specific specially designed todetect sentiments inasoftware engineering context andprovides incomparisontotheexistingdomain-independentsentimentanalysis

section,weprovideadescriptionofthedatasetalongwithdetailsoftheapproach conductsentimentanalysis.

collection

is apopular version control and project management system that provides multiple artifactsvizcommits,issues,andpullrequeststocontributors[32]. the data of seven GitHub projects. PostgreSQLis an open source RDBMS (relational database management system). Glibc is aGNUClibrarymostcommonlyusedbyGNU/Linuxsystem.Eclipse-CDTisanIDE (integrateddevelopment environment) fordeveloping programs inCandC++.GNUCash is accounting software developed forindividual andsmall businesses. WordPress isaPHP and MySQLbasedcontentmanagementsoftware.Firebugisawebbrowserextensionfor Mozilla Firefox.RhinoisanopensourceJavaScriptimplementationthatiscompletely written inJava.Generally,scriptingforendusersisimplementedinjavaapplication. 12We accessedtherepositoriesor git.ofThetheGitprojectsBashtoolfromGitHub was utilizedtocloneprojectrepositoriestothelocalmachine.Thecommitlogsofthe projects wereretrievedusingthegitlogcommand.Commitlogsofallselected wereanalyzed from their beginning totill February 2019. Incase ofFirebug ending period is October2017.

**3.2. Sentiment**analysis

Thereare a variety of sentiment analysis tools viz. SentiStrength [33], StafordNLP [34], and NLTK[35], while most ofthem donotfocus ontechnical text. Asthese tools aredesigned fornon-technical text such as movie reviews or blogs posted on social networking sites such as twitter,theirresultsareerroneousfortechnicalartifactsintheSoftwareEngineering (SE) domain[36].Therefore, domain-specific techniques provide betteraccuracy todetect sentimentsinsoftwareengineeringtext. WeusedsentimentanalysistoolSentiStrength-SEproposedbyIslamandZibran[15] to perform sentiment analysis on commit logs. Similar choice is made by Md Rakibul Islam and MinhazF.ZibraninSoftwareengineeringdomaintoextractemotionalscorefrom commit messages[7].UsingSentiStrengthasthebaseline,thistoolimplementsalexical based approachanddomainspecificdictionary.We selectedthistoolbecauseitisafirst SE specifictoolspecially designed forSoftware Engineering toconduct sentiment analysis and itoutperformstheexistingdomain-independent tools/toolkits[31].SentiStrength-SE tokenizesthe text into words and assigns ascore toeach word that conveys the underlying sentiment. Thewordswithpositive+1to+5sentimentandreceiveascorebetween words withnegative␀ 1to␀ 5 . Therangeneutralbetweenscoreofranges between+1to␀ 1 . Thescoringisgeneratedusingasentimentdictionarythatincludes the predeterminedpolarityscoreofsentimentwordsandphrases[32].Sentistrength-SE

areclassifiedassmall,projectswith61to200asmedium,and comprising morethan201developers aslarge(seeTable 4).Becher etal. astudy toanalyze number ofcontributors inarandom sample ofprojects included GNU/Linuxdistribution[38].We followedthepartitionproposedbyBecheretal. constructprojectsizeboundariesthatarepresentedinTable 4andcategorizethe into small, medium, andlargebasedonthesesizeboundaries [38].Thesentiment large,medium,andsmallprojectsispresentedinFigure3.

SentimentinprojectswithLarge,Medium,andSmallnumberofcontributor

Figure 3,all three categories (Large, Medium, and Small) ofprojects have count for neutral sentiments than negative and positive sentiments. The Projects with numberofcontributorshavemoreneutralsentiments(75.78%)ascomparedto having medium and small number of contributors. But, we see an opposite trend in having medium andsmall number ofcontributors. Inwithteam the percentage of positive sentiments (8.14%) expressed is higher and the sentiments islower than projects with small team size. Lastly,sentiment inprojectswithsmallteamsize. to the fluctuating number of team members in an OSS project over a period of time, worthytorelatesentimentsincommitlogswiththenumberofactivedevelopersin time. For this, we identified active developers intheprojects ineach year

2018whereasforFirebugitisSeptember2016.Developersthoseshowany afterJanuary2018isconsideredactive.IncaseofFirebugdevelopershavingany after September 2016 considered active. Sentiments are mapped to number of active in each year to determine the relation of sentiment with active(team arepresentedinFigure4.Asshownin4,findingsofEclipse-CDT, andfirebugindicatethatneutralsentimentsarehighwithlargeor team. researchquestion,wewanttodeterminetheassociationbetweensentiment commitcontribution.Inordertoachievethisgoal,weanalyzeddevelopers’ in commit logs and calculate thecontribution of the top ten contributors. isthepercentageofcommitsmadebyeachindividualcontributor Wecalculatethecommitcontributionbydividingthetotalcommitsof contributorbytotalnumberofcommitsmadeintheproject.Sentiment formulized in the same way as commit contribution size, is percentage of sentiments andNeutral)conveyedbyeachindividualdeveloperinthecommit alsocomputethesentimentvolumeofeachcontributorbydividingindividual sentiment(Positive,Negative,andNeutral)bysentimentsof formulasusedforcalculationofcommitcontributionandsentimentvolume mentionedbelow:

*Total**Commits**of**Individual**Contributors* *Commit Contribution*= *Total**Number**of**Commits**in**the**Project*

*Contributor Sentiment* *Sentiment Volume*= *Total**Sentimentin**the**Project*

*Contributor Total**Positive**Sentiment* *Volume=* *Total**Positive**Sentiments**of**Project*

*Contributor Total**Negative**Sentiment* *Sentiment**Volume=* *Total**Negative**Sentiments**of**Project*

*Contributor Total**Neutral**Sentiment* *Sentiment**Volume=* *Total**Neutral**Sentiments**of**Project*

**5. Discussion**

In this projects to objective contribution with the evolution of neutral negativity (36.03%) attributed to The the team have an evolution have in comparison to a project more developers that after in commit most of over the domain, that The study commit sentiments. As noticed commit log the sentiment of positive, the case less than analysis shows in this for an is required to After this –A project, contribution quality –In the into multiple clubbed with –Lead developers –Developers, looking sentiments

etal.e-InformaticaSoftware EngineeringJournal,16(2022),220102

work,wehaveexamined86,515commitmessagesofsevenwellknownGitHub analyzethesentiments expressedbydevelopersinthecommitlogs.Ourmain wastoinvestigate therelation ofteamsize,typeofchange activity, andcommit sentimentsinthecommitlogs.Inadditiontothis,wealsolookinto sentiment intheseprojects.We foundthatmostoftheprojectshadhigh sentiments in comparison to negative and positive ones. PostgreSQL indicates more insentimentsandmostoftheinthecommitlogscanbe leadingcodecontributors. majority ofthe commits inthe commit logs are neutral. Our findings revealed that sizeofaproject,typeofchangeactivity,anddevelopers’commitcontribution impactonthesentimentexpressedinthecommitlogs.Furthermore,duringthe oftheproject sentiments have different trends. We noticed that thecommit logs morepositive/negative andlessneutralsentiments intheinitialyearsoftheproject later years. Themain reason behind itmay bethat inthestarting years, islesscomplexandhaveasmallnumberofissuesbutastheprogresses, issues need to be resolved and large size of a team makes it a more formal platform and express themselves inaneutral way. Noticing the trend inFigure 7(b) indicates expression intheglibc project, prior to1990, was positive. Itstarted getting negative that.Ageormaturity ofaprojectdoesnotinfluence developer sentiment expression logs.Buttaking2008asthereferencepoint,whenGithubwaslaunchedand theprojectsmighthave shiftedtoGithub then,negative sentiment hasdecreased periodoftime.Soitmaybeduetoavailability ofthecommitlogsinthepublic sentimentexpressionhasbecomemorepositive. presentedbySinha[5]alsoexaminedthedevelopers’sentimentsinthe logs.Theyidentified thatthemajority ofGitHubcommits(74.74%) have neutral wecompareourfindingsofRQ1,withresultspresentedbySinha[5],we thatourwork foundsimilar results. We observed thatmostofthecommits inthe hadaneutralsentiment. To compareourresultswithSinha[5],wecombined resultsofallobservedprojectsandfoundthatinouranalysispercentage negative,andneutralsentimentsare4.73%,26.98%,68.29%,respectively. ofouranalysispositivesentimentsare2.47%andneutralare6.45% Sinha’sstudy.Negativesentimentsare8.93%higherthanThis thatthisresultisverysimilarforadatasetdifferentfromtheonestudied research.Theystartedwith28,466OSSprojectsbutconsideredonly5 in-depthsentimentanalysis.Sofardetailedanalysis,moreworkinthisdirection confirm the findings for OSS projects ofdifferent domains andsizes. analysis,someactionableadvicefortheOSScommunitycanbeasfollows: largeorsmall,shouldhaveacodeofconductmentioningthedesired incommitlogs. issuetrackingsystem,issuesinvolvingcomplexchangesshouldbedecomposed simpleissuesinvolvingonlytwoactivitiesi.e.modifyactivityshould eitheraddordeleteactivity. needtobeawareoftheirsentimentexpressions. forprojectstocontribute,canexpectbettercommitlogs,from pointofview,inmatureprojects.

be

<!-- Página 19 -->

Rajdeep Kauretal.e-InformaticaSoftware EngineeringJournal,16(2022),220102

**6. Threat**tovalidity

The authors examined developers’ sentiments insubjectlineofcommitmessage butbody of commit message may have different sentiments. For example, subject line may be neutral, but messagebodymaybenegativeorviceversa.Thisaspectismissinginthisstudy. Same developermayhaveregisteredwithmultiplenames.Multiplealiasesrelatedto same developersisnotresolvedthatmayinfluencethefindings. Moreover, theselectionoftheprojectsisbiasedasweincludedthehaving a validGitrepositorywhileprojectshostedonotherplatformslikeGitlabandBitbucket are nottakenintoconsideration.Asubsetoftheresearchquestionsexploredonalarge Github datasetin[5]alsogivesresultssimilartotheonesobtainedhere.Inthefuture, we willextendourdatasettoincludemoreprojectsthatarehostedonothersoftware repositories. Furthermore, theresultpresentedinthisstudyonlyappliestoOSSprojects. In RQ4,theauthorsincludedthedataofthetoptendeveloperswithveryhighcommit activitywhile developers with very low commit (commitless than 1%) activities are not included inouranalysis. Inthefuture, wewillextend ourstudytoinclude developers with lowlevelsofcontribution. Also combinationsadd +ofmodifyfilefile,deletechange+fileviz.and+ delete+modifyfile areconsideredbyauthorstoconductanalysiswhereasimpactof individualfile change (add, delete, and modify) is not explored. Further research is required to examine the impact ofindividual file change like addition, deletion, and modification on sentiments. Another limitationofourstudyisthatweconsideredonlyafewfactorstothe impact ofdevelopers’sentimentswhiletherearemanyotherfactorssuchascodequality, gender,project age, and popularity that may influence sentiments expressed inthe commit logs.

**7. Conclusions**

In thispaper,theauthorshaveanalyzedthedevelopers’sentimentsinthecommitlogs of OSSprojects.Weexamined86,515commitmessagesofthesevenmostpopularOSS projectsto analyze the sentiments expressed by developers in the commit logs. The authors investigatedthe impact of team size of the project, type of change activity (Type-1, Type-2, and Type-3)performedbydevelopers,andcodecontributionvolumetothesentiments expressed inthecommitlogs.Moreover,weanalyzedtheevolutionofsentimentsacross years withrespecttothenumberofcommitsmadeineachyear. Ourstudy reveals that themajority ofprojects have neutral sentiments. This indicates thatwhile creating commit log messages developers are more neutral. But when we compared negative withpositivesentiments,wefoundthatincaseofthreeprojects,percentage negativesentiment ismore than 10% greater than positive inall the projects, and sentimentis more than 23% higher than positive in four projects. In this study, we perceived that sentimentsinthecommitlogsareinfluencedbyteamsize.Neutralexpressionsare high withlargeteamsizeandnegativeexpressionsarehighwithsmallteamsize.The typeofchange activity performed bydevelopers alsoinfluences their sentiments expressed in thecommitlogs.Type-3activityinvolvingallthethreechangeactionsofaddition, deletion,andmodification, indicates more negative sentiments andlow neutral sentiments. Furthermore,wealsonoticedthatcontribution sizealsoimpactsthevolume ofsentiment.

220102-19

of
